summary: The emissions/mint/settlement spine is split across four repos with a clean conceptual handoff but a leaky, drift-prone implementation. afi-math owns the off-chain 'canonical' three-phase 86B emissions schedule (pure, deterministic, float-based); afi-mint coordinates per-signal minting by computing a token amount from a copy of that schedule (inlined, not imported) and invoking the on-chain AFIMintCoordinator.mintForSignal; afi-token executes the mint, enforcing only the 86B hard cap and an EMISSIONS_ROLE gate. Mints are per-signal (one tx, one beneficiary each), not epoch-batch — the epoch only appears as a budget input to off-chain proportional distribution and as an event field. The on-chain reality is a single-beneficiary mint with no schedule/epoch-cap/score enforcement, while the multi-role gauge 'splits' model lives only in the research repo afi-econ and the goldpaper formula. In production (Pattern A), both DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE and EMISSIONS_ROLE are granted to one Treasury Safe (the authority/role-holder), whereas the mint recipient is an arbitrary per-signal beneficiary address supplied off-chain — the Treasury Safe is not the recipient.

answers.question: Trace the emissions handoff: how does the emissions schedule flow afi-math -> afi-mint -> afi-token contracts?
answers.answer: afi-math defines the schedule: emissionsSchedule.ts is the self-described 'Canonical implementation of the three-phase front-loaded emissions model' distributing an 86,000,000,000 cap over ~53 years, exposing buildEmissionsSchedule/getEpochEmission/getRemainingSupply as pure float math (base units, not wei). afi-mint does NOT import this; EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts re-declares the same schedule code 'Inlined from afi-math to avoid circular dependency issues' (cap: 86_000_000_000n) and uses getEpochEmission(schedule, epoch) to obtain an epoch budget, then derives a per-signal bigint tokenAmount. MintExecutor.buildMintRequest packages {beneficiary, tokenAmount, receiptId, receiptAmount, signalId, epoch, extraData} and calls IMintCoordinatorContract.mintForSignal. On-chain, AFIMintCoordinator.mintForSignal (onlyRole(EMISSIONS_ROLE)) forwards to AFIToken.mintEmissions(beneficiary, tokenAmount), which is the ONLY emissions entrypoint and enforces just the hard cap. The token contract holds NO schedule/epoch logic — the comment confirms 'Epoch-specific logic and DAG/TSSD integration are handled at the coordinator/off-chain policy layers, NOT in this token contract.' So the schedule is authoritative only off-chain (and duplicated), while the chain enforces a single 86B ceiling.

answers.question: Is minting per-signal or epoch-batch — and how does the implemented behavior compare to what is documented?
answers.answer: Implemented behavior is strictly PER-SIGNAL: AFIMintCoordinator.mintForSignal takes one MintRequest (one signalId, one beneficiary, one epoch) per call, and off-chain MintExecutor.processReadySignals iterates ready signals 'for (const signal of readyForMint)' calling executeMint -> mintForSignal once per signal; ValidatorDaemon runs this each cycle. There is no batch/multicall mint of many signals in one transaction (the only 'batch' constructs are reputation weighting and Snapshot challenge proposals, not minting). The EPOCH appears only as (a) a budget input — getEpochEmission returns the epoch's emission budget, which is then split proportionally across that epoch's signals (proportionalShare = signalWeight / totalWeight) — and (b) an event/breadcrumb field (MintCoordinated.epochId, MintRequest.epoch). So 'epoch' is a documented accounting/budget concept; the documented goldpaper 'E_epoch' Epoch-Pulse batch rhythm is realized as per-signal proportional draws against an epoch budget, not as a single epoch settlement transaction. The afi-econ research kit models a true epoch-level settlement (compute_epoch_payouts/apply_gauge over a pool) but that is simulation-only and not in the production path.

answers.question: What is the beneficiary model — single payee vs split — and where are splits documented (afi-econ / token docs / whitepaper)?
answers.answer: The implemented/on-chain beneficiary model is SINGLE-PAYEE: MintRequest carries exactly one 'beneficiary' and one 'tokenAmount'; mintForSignal mints all tokens to req.beneficiary (and an optional single ERC-1155 receipt to the same address). There is no split/gauge/multi-beneficiary distribution anywhere in afi-mint or afi-token (grep for split|gauge|beneficiaries returns no minting logic). MULTI-ROLE SPLITS exist only as documentation/research: the afi-econ research kit models role-based gauge allocation (producers 0.55 / enrichment 0.25 / validators 0.10 / public_goods 0.10, per_role_max cap, policy/merit blend) via apply_gauge/compute_epoch_payouts, but afi-econ is explicitly 'Research-Grade / Placeholder Models ... Do NOT use for ... Whitepaper claims (formulas are not final) ... Smart contract configuration', informs contract parameters only ('afi-econ models inform contract parameters'), and has no code/runtime dependency from the contracts. The whitepaper/goldpaper splits framing therefore has no normative or on-chain realization — the protocol mints to a single per-signal beneficiary.

answers.question: Treasury Safe roles vs mint recipient: what is the production truth on who holds authority and who receives minted tokens?
answers.answer: They are DISTINCT and must not be conflated. Authority: the mainnet deploy script uses 'Pattern A' role wiring where BOTH DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE and EMISSIONS_ROLE are granted to a single AFI_TREASURY_SAFE (Gnosis Safe), and the deployer EOA receives no roles; post-deploy the Safe grants token.EMISSIONS_ROLE to the coordinator and coordinator.EMISSIONS_ROLE to an emissions agent. So the Treasury Safe is the on-chain policy owner and root mint authority — the only thing gating emissions legitimacy is holding EMISSIONS_ROLE (mintEmissions is onlyRole(EMISSIONS_ROLE) with no schedule/score/epoch-cap check beyond the 86B ceiling). Recipient: the minted tokens go to req.beneficiary — an arbitrary address resolved per signal from off-chain metadata (EmissionsMintDataProvider.getBeneficiary -> signal metadata.beneficiary), NOT to the Treasury Safe. In short: Treasury Safe = role-holder/authority (and admin), per-signal beneficiary = recipient; the production truth is that a single Safe authorizes mints to externally-specified recipients, and a third party cannot verify schedule/score adherence from chain data alone.

answers.question: Is the documented goldpaper mint formula implemented as written, and is the off-chain schedule the on-chain authority?
answers.answer: No on both counts. The goldpaper formula is documented in EmissionsMintDataProvider as 'DeltaAFI_i = clamp(B(t) x Q_i x N_i x R_val,i x E_epoch, 0.5xB(t), 2.0xB(t))', but the implementation does NOT use the B(t) base-multiplier/clamp form: it computes signalWeight = Q*N*R, takes a proportional share of the remaining epoch budget (baseAmount = remainingBudget * signalWeight/totalWeight), multiplies by epochPulseFactor, floors to wei, then clamps to fixed min/max token bounds — explicitly noting 'This replaces the B(t) multiplier with actual budget-based allocation'. The baseMultiplier B(t)=8 constant is defined but unused in the amount path. Separately, the off-chain schedule is NOT the on-chain authority: afi-math's AGENTS guidance says afi-token 'may reference emissions curves from afi-math (for off-chain simulation)', the schedule is float base-units (precision-lossy vs wei), and the only on-chain emissions constraint is TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP — the per-epoch schedule is never enforced on-chain.

findings.title: Canonical emissions schedule is copy-pasted (inlined) into afi-mint instead of imported, creating drift risk in the replay-critical mint path
findings.evidence: afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:19-20 'Inlined from afi-math to avoid circular dependency issues. Canonical source: afi-math/src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts'; duplicate constants afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:51 'cap: 86_000_000_000n' vs afi-math/src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts:60 'cap: 86_000_000_000n'
findings.recommendation: Replace the inlined copy with a versioned import of afi-math, and pin the afi-math emissions ruleset version in the mint receipt so the exact schedule used for an amount is reproducible. Otherwise the off-chain 'canonical' schedule and its mint-time copy can silently diverge.

findings.title: Documented goldpaper mint formula is not the implemented formula (B(t) clamp model replaced by proportional epoch-budget allocation)
findings.evidence: afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:10-12 'DeltaAFI_i = clamp(B(t) x Q_i x N_i x R_val,i x E_epoch, 0.5xB(t), 2.0xB(t))' vs afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:277-281 'const proportionalShare = signalWeight / totalWeight; const baseAmount = remainingBudget * proportionalShare; ... const adjustedAmount = baseAmount * this.config.epochPulseFactor;' (baseMultiplier B(t)=8 at :202 is unused in the amount path)
findings.recommendation: Reconcile the docstring/goldpaper with the code: either implement the clamp(0.5xB(t),2xB(t)) form or rewrite the documented formula to the proportional-share-of-epoch-budget model actually used. An external validator reproducing mint amounts from the documented formula will not match on-chain mints.

findings.title: Snapshot governance vote and reputation/epoch-pulse multipliers can override the deterministic validator decision and mint amount
findings.evidence: afi-mint/src/orchestrator/SignalStateManager.ts:284-286 'if (challengeSucceeded) { ... finalDecision = state.validatorDecision === \'qualified\' ? \'reject\' : \'mint\'; }'; afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:272-281 'const R = metadata.reputationWeight; const signalWeight = Q * N * R; ... const adjustedAmount = baseAmount * this.config.epochPulseFactor;'
findings.recommendation: Per north-star rule 5, reputation/governance must influence selection/allocation but MUST NOT override deterministic scoring/finality. Document whether a governance challenge overturning a deterministic decision (qualified->reject / rejected->mint) and reputation/epochPulse scaling of the minted amount are intended; if so, classify it explicitly as appeal-of-record, not deterministic finality.

findings.title: On-chain emissions legitimacy is gated only by EMISSIONS_ROLE (held by one Treasury Safe); no schedule/epoch-cap/score is enforced on-chain
findings.evidence: afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:92 'function mintEmissions(address beneficiary, uint256 amount) external onlyRole(EMISSIONS_ROLE)'; afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:97 'if (totalSupply() + amount > TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP)'; afi-token/script/DeployAFITokenMainnet.s.sol:60-63 'address admin_ = treasurySafe; address emissionsController_ = treasurySafe;'
findings.recommendation: Either anchor the per-epoch schedule/cap (or a signed schedule commitment) on-chain, or explicitly document that emissions adherence is a trusted off-chain policy enforced by the Treasury Safe and cannot be independently verified from chain data. The only on-chain invariant today is the 86B total cap.

findings.title: On-chain mint is single-beneficiary; the multi-role gauge/splits model exists only in research (afi-econ) with no contract or production path
findings.evidence: afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:19-27 'struct MintRequest { address beneficiary; uint256 tokenAmount; ... }' and :76 'token.mintEmissions(req.beneficiary, req.tokenAmount);'; afi-econ/params/gauge_v0.yaml:4-11 'producers: 0.55 enrichment: 0.25 validators: 0.10 public_goods: 0.10'; afi-econ/README.md:24-26 'Do NOT use for: Production parameter decisions ... Whitepaper claims ... Smart contract configuration'
findings.recommendation: Resolve the splits-vs-single-beneficiary tension in the normative spec: either define a normative split/gauge distribution that the coordinator must implement, or document that role-based splits are research-only and the protocol mints to a single beneficiary per signal.

findings.title: Off-chain mint_receipt_schema diverges from the on-chain MintRequest and lacks epoch/beneficiary/version/content-hash anchoring
findings.evidence: afi-mint/codex/mint_receipt_schema.json:6-13 required ['signal_id','score','mint_amount','validator_id','challenge_status','timestamp'] vs afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:19-27 'MintRequest { beneficiary; tokenAmount; receiptId; receiptAmount; signalId; epoch; extraData }'
findings.recommendation: Align the off-chain receipt with MintRequest and add determinism-anchoring fields (epoch, beneficiary, emissions ruleset version, payload/content hash) so a mint can be bound to its inputs for challenge-ready replay.

findings.title: Emissions schedule uses floating-point base units for token accounting (precision loss vs wei/bigint)
findings.evidence: afi-math/src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts:144 'const emissions = baseEmissions.map(e => e * scale);' (number[] floats) and :41 '/** Per-epoch emission amounts (in base units, not wei) */'; afi-mint conversion afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:284 'BigInt(Math.floor(adjustedAmount * 10 ** this.config.decimals))'
findings.recommendation: If schedule outputs ever feed authoritative on-chain accounting, define a deterministic float->integer (wei) quantization rule; float drift across stacks breaks replay determinism of minted amounts.

findings.title: Minting is per-signal (one tx per signal), not an epoch-batch settlement; 'epoch' is only a budget input and event field
findings.evidence: afi-mint/src/orchestrator/MintExecutor.ts:74-91 'const readyForMint = await this.stateManager.getReadyForMinting(); for (const signal of readyForMint) { await this.executeMint(signal); }'; afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:85 'emit MintCoordinated(req.signalId, req.epoch, ...)'
findings.recommendation: Document the settlement model explicitly: per-signal mints drawing proportionally from an epoch budget, distinct from the afi-econ research model's epoch-pool gauge settlement, so readers do not assume a single epoch batch transaction.

contradictions.doc_says: Token emissions are distributed across multiple roles via a gauge (producers 0.55 / enrichment 0.25 / validators 0.10 / public_goods 0.10) with per-role caps and merit blending.
contradictions.code_does: The on-chain coordinator mints all tokens for a signal to a single beneficiary address; there is no split/gauge/multi-beneficiary logic in afi-mint or afi-token.

contradictions.doc_says: Per-signal token amount follows the goldpaper formula DeltaAFI_i = clamp(B(t) x Q_i x N_i x R_val,i x E_epoch, 0.5xB(t), 2.0xB(t)).
contradictions.code_does: The implementation ignores B(t) for the amount and instead allocates a proportional share of the remaining epoch budget (remainingBudget * signalWeight/totalWeight) times epochPulseFactor, then clamps to fixed min/max token bounds.

contradictions.doc_says: afi-math's emissionsSchedule.ts is the 'Canonical implementation' / canonical source of the emissions model for the ecosystem.
contradictions.code_does: afi-mint re-declares the schedule inline ('to avoid circular dependency') rather than importing it, and afi-token enforces no schedule at all (only the 86B cap), so the 'canonical' source is duplicated and not on-chain authoritative.

contradictions.doc_says: A governance/Snapshot challenge and reputation weighting feed selection and allocation only, and must not override deterministic scoring or protocol finality.
contradictions.code_does: A successful challenge overturns the deterministic validator decision (qualified->reject / rejected->mint), and reputationWeight + governance epochPulseFactor scale the minted amount.

contradictions.doc_says: The on-chain commitment plane (afi-token/afi-mint) is the authority for emissions caps and epoch linkage.
contradictions.code_does: Only the 86B TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP is enforced on-chain; emissions legitimacy is gated solely by EMISSIONS_ROLE held by a single Treasury Safe (Pattern A), with the per-epoch schedule and score checks living entirely off-chain.
